Life Sketch of Richard Treat
memoryArtifacts.description:
Richard was born about 1508 probably in Staplegrove, Somerset, England.  He was the son of William Treat.  He had an older and a younger brother, and three younger sisters.

     Richard married a …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Trott於1508年出生在英國薩默塞特斯塔普萊格洛福時，他的父親William Trott28歲，母親Mrs Joann Trott26歲。他於1536年與Joanna Scadding在英國薩默塞特斯塔普萊格洛福結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1570年11月在英國薩默塞特比特明斯特享年62歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特奧特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Trott于1508年出生在英国薩默塞特斯塔普萊格洛福时，他的父亲William Trott28岁，母亲Mrs Joann Trott26岁。他于1536年与Joanna Scadding在英国薩默塞特斯塔普萊格洛福结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1570年11月在英国薩默塞特比特明斯特享年62岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特奧特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Trott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset and Devon): in southwestern England, usually a variant of   Trout  .
South German: metonymic variant of   Trotter   2.

English (Somerset and Devon): nickname, either for one who moves quickly, at the pace of a trotting horse, from Middle English trot(te) (Old French trot) ‘trot’, perhaps given to a messenger (see Trotter , Trotman ); or occasionally perhaps from Middle English trot(te), trat(te) ‘old woman, hag’ (Anglo-Norman French trote).

variants.name: Trout
meanings: English (mainly Devon): nickname from Middle English trout(e), trut(e), trought(e), trot ‘trout’ (Old English trūht), perhaps for someone who caught or sold trout, or who was thought to resemble the fish in some way.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Trott was born in 1508, in Staplegrove, Somerset, England, his father, William Trott, was 28 and his mother, Mrs Joann Trott, was 26. He married Joanna Scadding in 1536, in Staplegrove, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in November 1570, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Otterford, Somerset, England.

Il a épousé Joanna Scadding en 1536, à Staplegrove, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en novembre 1570, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Otterford, Somerset, Angleterre.

Er hat 1536 in Staplegrove, Somerset, England Joanna Scadding geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. ko.lifeSummary: Richard Trott(이)가 1508년 영국 서머세트 스타플레그로브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Trott(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Joann Trott(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1536년 영국 서머세트 스타플레그로브에서 Joanna Scadding(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1570년 11월 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 오터퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.St John's Church, Skilgate, Somerset, En: ст джон'с чурч, скилгэит, сомирсет, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Trott родился в 1508 г. в местности стаплегров, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени William Trott было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Joann Trott, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna Scadding в 1536 г. в местности стаплегров, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер ноябрь 1570 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности оттирфорд, сомирсет, Англия.
